home.name: Xingxing Song
home.intro: Welcome to my personal website. I'm a sophomore at Beijing Jiaotong University majoring in AI, focused on LLM training and Agent research, striving toward AGI. Here you can explore my academic background, research projects, and tech stack. Feel free to use the AI Chat to learn more about me.
home.aiCta: Want to know more? Try AI Chat
home.aiCtaSub: Ask questions in natural language — AI will answer based on my profile

about.title: About Me
paragraphs: I'm Xingxing Song, a sophomore majoring in Artificial Intelligence at Beijing Jiaotong University (Class of 2024). I focus on LLM Training and Agent research, devoted to achieving Artificial General Intelligence (AGI).
I'm passionate about AI and coding, fascinated by the logical beauty of code. I feel the pulse of AI in the human world — the emergence of silicon-based life.

I'm a sunny, cheerful, and enthusiastic person. Calm, independent, and open-minded. I love sports — gym, swimming, running, tennis, hiking, baseball, badminton. I also love traveling — I've been to Suzhou, Shanghai, Xiamen, Ordos, Hulunbuir, Harbin, and my next destination is California.

items.desc: School of Computer Science. Courses: Reinforcement Learning, Algorithm Design & Analysis, AI Mathematical Foundations, Data Structures, Calculus, Linear Algebra, Probability Theory.

items.title: GUITestBench: GUI Agent Benchmark
items.desc: Co-proposed the first systematic benchmark for evaluating GUI Agents' autonomous defect detection, covering 18 real open-source Apps and 143 GitHub Issue-driven defect reproduction tasks. Designed the LLM-as-Judge framework with multi-source verdict methods and Defect Recall/Precision/F1 metrics.

items.title: Explainable Video Forensics Framework
items.desc: As a core member (team of 2), designed MCP-based heterogeneous tool-chain orchestration, researched MoE expert orthogonalization across physics, semantics, and spatiotemporal consistency, and co-built chain-of-thought structured n-tuple explainable forensic output.

items.title: RISC-V Rust Lightweight RTOS
items.desc: Built a lightweight RTOS kernel from scratch in Rust on RISC-V 64-bit, implementing preemptive multitasking, atomic synchronization primitives, 8 syscalls, and UART/VGA/PCI drivers. Responsible for compiler toolchain adaptation and filesystem development.
